title: E2E suite red on main: schedule-config nav, program-mgmt, publish-gate
description:
E2E (parallel + sequential) jobs failed on main since 2026-05-27 across 3+ commits. Failures span unrelated specs:
- e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts TC-007-01: Navigate to configuration page (30s timeout, 3 retries)
- e2e/09-program-management.spec.ts TC-PROG-050: heading structure
- e2e/16-publish-gate.spec.ts: should prevent publishing of incomplete semester
- Plus many '⚠️ No draggable elements found' warnings throughout

Lint hotfix in PR #223 did not touch these specs and was unrelated. Bug is pre-existing.

Symptoms look like navigation timeouts on dashboard pages and possibly seed or app startup drift since 2026-05-27. Investigate first: 03-schedule-config TC-007-01 timeout (most consistent). Run pnpm test:e2e locally to repro.
design:
Investigation 2026-05-29:

ROOT CAUSE: Tests pre-redesign, pages mid-redesign. Not a single regression — chronic drift since UI refactors started.

EVIDENCE:
- Schedule config: e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts TC-007-01 expects Thai labels 'กำหนดคาบต่อวัน', 'กำหนดระยะเวลาต่อคาบ', etc. None of those strings exist in src/ — page was refactored from form to ConfigSummaryClient (read-only summary) when wizard absorbed initial config (commit 4e26f832 on 2026-05-27).
- Public schedule pages: e2e/public-schedule-pages.spec.ts uses selectors 'td .font-medium', '.text-xs' assuming <table>-based layout. 4a0 redesign plan Tasks 6-7 migrate to TimeslotGrid (<div>-based card layout). Selectors won't survive migration.
- Orphan files: src/app/(public)/teachers/[id]/[academicYear]/[semester]/ has 3 variants (page.tsx + page-new.tsx + page-grid.tsx) — partial migration started outside the official plan.
- Visual snapshots: pre-redesign baselines.

REMEDIATION PATH (recommended):
- Do NOT do a big-bang test rewrite.
- Scope new E2E coverage under each migration task in the 4a0 plan:
  - Task 6 PR (public teacher schedule → TimeslotGrid): rewrite public-schedule-pages.spec.ts teacher tests + clean orphan page variants.
  - Task 7 PR (public class schedule): rewrite class portions of public-schedule-pages.spec.ts and public-data-api.spec.ts.
  - Task 8 PR (dashboard all-timeslot): rewrite e2e/smoke/semester-smoke.spec.ts dashboard tests + critical-ui.spec.ts dashboard snapshots.
  - Tasks 11-13 PRs: rewrite TeachersTableClient / ClassesTableClient list tests.
- Separately (not 4a0 scope): rewrite e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts to test ConfigSummaryClient read-only summary view. File as new bug. The current spec was correct for the old form, but that UI no longer exists.

DO NOTS:
- Don't 'fix' tests by relaxing selectors generically — that just hides the next regression.
- Don't regenerate visual snapshot baselines until the redesign is complete (Task 14+).
- Don't merge any orphan page-*.tsx variants without claiming them in a task PR.
acceptance_criteria: E2E parallel + sequential jobs green on main and PR branches
notes: Real-bugs-only pass (user scope) 2026-05-29. FIXED 2 user-facing server crashes from CI run 26630925367: (1) RSC crash component={Link} in arrange/layout.tsx empty-state -> PR #234; (2) /api/schedule/teacher/[id] 500 on non-numeric teacher id (parseInt->NaN->'Argument TeacherID is missing') -> 400 guard + numeric caller guard, PR #235 (covers the 7x class_schedule.findMany validation errors = same route). NOT-A-BUG: class_schedule.create 'Unique constraint (TimeslotID,GradeID)' = data-integrity safety net working under concurrent-placement race; ActionWrapper returns {success:false} gracefully (not a 500 page) + logs noise. RESIDUAL (follow-up, same NaN-teacher family): teachers_responsibility.findMany 'TeacherID missing' via TeacherActions + timeslot.findMany validation (2x) — lower frequency, need action->repo tracing; apply same NaN-guard pattern. DOMINANT redness remains selector drift (~40 specs) + stale visual snapshots — deferred per 8kp incremental strategy (redesign dn3/i8z still in flight).

title: Fix N+1 in public-data.repository getRoomOccupancy/getPeriodLoad
description: Public homepage MiniCharts triggers ~40-55 sequential prisma counts (1 per timeslot in getRoomOccupancy + 1 per weekday in getPeriodLoad). Each round-trips remote Accelerate -> dev hangs/slow page load. Replace with single findMany + in-memory tally and Promise.all parallelization.
close_reason: Fixed in 10ca9791 (N+1 collapse) + b5bc90ea (local docker DB swap)

title: E2E test env leaks PRISMA_DATABASE_URL from .env, routes seed/tests at remote DB
description: prisma/seed.ts:76 `import "dotenv/config"` auto-loads .env, which sets PRISMA_DATABASE_URL pointing at the remote Prisma Postgres / Accelerate database. prismaClientSingleton() in src/lib/prisma.ts prefers PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL over the locally-set DATABASE_URL when present, so e2e seeds and the test web server were silently running against the remote production-like DB. Symptom: seed fails with USER_ALREADY_EXISTS_USE_ANOTHER_EMAIL even though local test DB is empty (`docker exec timetable-test-db psql ...` returns 0 rows in User). Fix: add empty PRISMA_DATABASE_URL= and ACCELERATE_URL= overrides to .env.test and .env.test.local. Follow-up: consider gating the Accelerate selection in prismaClientSingleton on NODE_ENV !== 'test' to make this trap impossible.
close_reason: Fixed in 483dd8f0: explicit empty PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL in .env.test prevents .env leakage from routing seeds at remote Prisma Postgres. 12/12 teaching-assignment e2e specs now pass.

title: Remove 'as any' on Prisma groupBy in assign + dashboard repos
description: Two live-code type escapes bypass Prisma type safety: src/features/assign/infrastructure/repositories/assign.repository.ts:106 and src/features/dashboard/infrastructure/repositories/dashboard.repository.ts:351 both cast prisma.<table> as any to call .groupBy(). Brittle against Prisma upgrades. Action: investigate why typed groupBy fails (missing select? Prisma client regen needed?), fix root cause, drop casts.
close_reason: Dropped (prisma.model as any).groupBy() in both repos. Root cause: as T[] result cast triggered backward inference through Prisma InputErrors conditional type. Fix: fully inferred return type, removed unused local type aliases.

title: Arrange: default room per teacher+subject — auto-assign on drop
description: Feature B (brainstormed 2026-06-01). Let a user set a default room for a teacher's subject so future drops skip the room picker. Scope = per teacher+subject (decision) = a teachers_responsibility row (unique key TeacherID+GradeID+SubjectCode+AcademicYear+Semester). Behavior decision = auto-assign (skip picker) when a default is set and the room is free; if the default room is busy at that timeslot, fall back to opening the room modal (Fork 2 = open modal to pick).
design: SCHEMA: add teachers_responsibility.DefaultRoomID Int? + relation to room (room.RoomID PK). One prisma migration (pnpm db:migrate). SET: add a checkbox in RoomSelectionContent — 'ตั้งเป็นห้องเริ่มต้นสำหรับวิชานี้'. On confirm-with-checkbox, persist alongside create: extend createClassScheduleAction input with optional SetAsDefaultRoom:boolean; when true, update the resp row's DefaultRoomID to the chosen RoomID (single round-trip; resp id already passed as ResponsibilityIDs). USE: the palette query that builds subject items (carries RespID, data-resp-id/data-subject-code) must also expose DefaultRoomID; add DefaultRoomID to SubjectDragData payload. In ArrangeDndProvider.handleDragEnd, after validateDropAction allowed: if subjectData.DefaultRoomID set -> check the room is free at the timeslot; if free, call createClassScheduleAction directly with RoomID=DefaultRoomID (skip setRoomModal) + dispatch 'schedule-updated' + router.refresh(); if busy, open the room modal as today (default shown disabled). ROOM-FREE CHECK: extend validateDropAction to optionally accept roomId and report a 'room_conflict' reason (keep conflict logic server-side, one place); auto-assign path passes the default roomId. DEPENDS ON ed6 (modal-only) so RoomSelectionContent lives at arrange/_components and the modal is the single path.
acceptance_criteria: Picking a room with the checkbox ticked persists DefaultRoomID on that teachers_responsibility row. Dropping the same subject again on a free timeslot creates the entry with the default room and NO modal opens. Dropping where the default room is busy opens the modal with the default disabled. Removing the only-default-using path leaves existing no-default drops opening the modal as before. pnpm build + typecheck + relevant e2e pass.

title: Arrange: room-select modal-only — remove vestigial standalone + intercept routes
description: Feature A (brainstormed 2026-06-01). The live room picker is the client-state <Dialog> in ArrangeDndProvider (opens from setRoomModal on drop; renders RoomSelectionContent). The standalone route src/app/schedule/[ay]/[sem]/arrange/room-select/page.tsx and the intercepting route @modal/(.)room-select/page.tsx are vestigial — NOTHING in src/ navigates to /arrange/room-select (grep: only ArrangeDndProvider + the (.)route itself reference room-select). Leftovers from an earlier URL-driven modal replaced by the client Dialog. User decision: room selection should ONLY ever be an overlay on arrange — remove the standalone entirely.
design: DELETE: room-select/page.tsx; @modal/(.)room-select/page.tsx; the whole @modal slot (@modal/default.tsx) + 'modal' prop and {modal} render in arrange/layout.tsx (no pages remain in the slot). MOVE: room-select/_components/RoomSelectionContent.tsx -> arrange/_components/RoomSelectionContent.tsx (room-select/ folder fully removed); update import in ArrangeDndProvider.tsx. SIMPLIFY: RoomSelectionContent currently branches onClose (inline) vs router.back() (routed). After removal there is no routed version — always call onClose; drop the router.back() branch and the useRouter import if now unused (keep router.refresh()). E2E (Fork 1 = real drag-drop): rewrite the two specs that goto the standalone URL — 21-arrangement-flow.spec.ts:163 (AR-ROOM) and tests/admin/schedule-assignment.spec.ts:198 (save) — to use ArrangePage.dragSubjectToTimeslot -> client modal -> select room (helper already used by passing conflict tests; 'flaky drag-drop' comment is likely stale). Verify pnpm build + the two specs locally (needs Docker Desktop running for test DB — was DOWN during this session, blocking e2e).
acceptance_criteria: No room-select/ folder and no @modal slot under arrange/. /arrange/room-select returns 404. Dropping a subject opens the room modal overlay (unchanged UX). pnpm build + pnpm typecheck pass. 21-arrangement-flow AR-ROOM and schedule-assignment save specs pass via drag-drop (Docker up).

title: BUG: arrange @grid blank on hard-nav/refresh after room-select (default.tsx returns null)
description:
Root-caused during a5l C9 local repro (2026-06-01). The arrange grid is a parallel-route slot (src/app/schedule/[ay]/[sem]/arrange/@grid). @grid/page.tsx renders GridSlot (the timetable table); @grid/default.tsx returns null. On a HARD navigation to /arrange (browser refresh, bookmark, or page.goto) that arrives AFTER the route has been on the room-select sub-route, Next renders the slot's default.tsx (null) instead of page.tsx — so the timetable grid is BLANK. Soft (client) navigation re-matches page.tsx and works, which is why normal click-through and AR-ROOM (modal router.back) are fine.

USER IMPACT: refreshing or deep-linking the arrange page after using room-select shows an empty grid until a soft re-nav.

TEST IMPACT: blocks e2e schedule-assignment :138 (remove) + :222 (save) which hard page.goto() back to /arrange after room-select (PR #247, a5l C9). Confirmed deterministic; waitForAppReady does not help (grid genuinely absent).

FIX (verify Next16 parallel-route default.tsx semantics via next-devtools nextjs_docs first): make @grid/default.tsx render <GridSlot/> (same as page.tsx) so hard-nav/refresh shows the grid. Check the other arrange slots (@palette/@inspector/@header default.tsx) for the same gap. Alternative narrow option: a children page.tsx. After fix, re-run e2e/tests/admin/schedule-assignment.spec.ts (Basic Operations) + e2e/21-arrangement-flow.spec.ts to confirm green.

ACCEPTANCE: hard refresh of /schedule/2568/1/arrange?teacher=X (and after a room-select round-trip) renders the populated timetable-grid; schedule-assignment :138/:222 pass.
notes: 2026-06-01 REFRAMED. Original mechanism (hard-nav back to /arrange renders @grid/default.tsx=null) is WRONG: page.goto is a full load with no client state to recover, so /arrange always matches @grid/page.tsx (verified vs Next16 default.js docs). Also the room picker is already a client-state Dialog in ArrangeDndProvider (not URL-driven); @modal/(.)room-select + standalone room-select/page.tsx are vestigial dead routes. The schedule-assignment e2e 'failure' this session reproduced only as a Docker-Desktop-DOWN env error in global-setup (docker compose could not start test DB), NOT a routing bug. Superseded by ed6 (remove standalone, modal-only).

title: Perf: analytics + conflicts pages slow (~8-12s app-code) — likely e2e/CI flake source
description:
Found during a5l local e2e repro (2026-05-31). The dashboard analytics and conflicts pages are slow to render server-side, which makes their e2e specs marginal against 15s timeouts and is a probable contributor to chronic 8kp CI flakiness.

EVIDENCE (local dev:e2e run, docker pg :5433):
- GET /dashboard/2568/1/analytics: ~12.5s total, next.js ~3.9s, application-code ~8.5s.
- GET /dashboard/2568/1/conflicts: similar; on cold client-nav the heading did not render within the 15s assertion timeout (a5l C6 :19) — fixed in PR #253 by adding waitForAppReady, but that masks the underlying slowness rather than fixing it.
- Dev mode (on-demand compile) inflates first-hit numbers, but ~8.5s of pure application-code on analytics is the real signal, not just compile.

LIKELY CAUSES TO INVESTIGATE:
- analytics overview aggregations run many sequential COUNT queries (teachers_responsibility, timeslot, room, etc. — visible as repeated prisma:query COUNT in WebServer logs); candidate for batching / parallel Promise.all / a single aggregate query.
- conflict detection recomputed per request with no caching.

SCOPE: server-side data/query perf only. The e2e timing was already mitigated in PR #253 (waitForAppReady). This issue is about the actual page latency.

ACCEPTANCE: analytics + conflicts app-code render time materially reduced (target <2s app-code on warm dev); verify via WebServer GET timing logs and a production build run.

title: Arrange palette (รายวิชาที่สอน) stale after deleting a placed subject
description: @grid handleRemoveEntry (arrange/@grid/page.tsx) calls grid SWR mutate() + dispatches 'schedule-updated' but NOT router.refresh(). PaletteClient is server-prop-driven (no SWR/listener) and since PR #240 hides fully-placed subjects, so a deleted subject should reappear in the palette but doesn't update until refresh. title: E2E greening (C-run): analytics-overview, conflict-detector, auto-arrange, schedule-assignment (needs local repro)
description: Part of 8kp bucket C (run 26693931684). Cluster needing a local dev:e2e repro to split real-bug vs drift/seed: (C5) e2e/dashboard/analytics-overview-conflicts.spec.ts ×2 — fail at goto('/analytics') line, testids (conflict-stat-card/overview-stat-card-N in OverviewSection.tsx) exist, so PAGE fails to load/app-ready hangs → possible real analytics route bug. (C6) e2e/12-conflict-detector.spec.ts ×3 — 'navigate from dashboard' fails on the /Dashboard/i heading (dashboard-redesign drift); 'switch tabs'(81) + 'maintain tab on reload'(222) fail on conflicts-page room/no-conflict text (content/seed). (C8) e2e/22-auto-arrange.spec.ts:14 'button disabled when no teacher' fails at arrange page load (stack :17), blocking serial siblings — possible #240 arrange-grid regression. (C9) e2e/tests/admin/schedule-assignment.spec.ts:136 'remove subject from timeslot' ×3 ~55s timeouts (element never appears). Run each locally, then file/fix per finding.
notes: C9 ROOT CAUSE NAILED 2026-06-01 (local repro, #247 branch). schedule-assignment :138 (remove) + :222 (save) fail deterministically (warm+cold) at 'expect(timetable-grid).toBeVisible()' after the test hard page.goto()s BACK to /arrange following a visit to the room-select sub-route. PROOF: src/app/schedule/[ay]/[sem]/arrange/@grid/default.tsx = 'return null'. On hard nav to /arrange AFTER room-select, Next renders the @grid slot's default.tsx (null) instead of @grid/page.tsx (GridSlot with the table+testids), so timetable-grid never mounts and the grid data APIs (/api/timeslots,/api/schedule/teacher) never fire. Selectors/testids are CORRECT (DroppableCell emits timeslot-card/data-timeslot-id/data-subject-code; table has data-testid=timetable-grid). Confirmed NOT a stale-selector issue and NOT mere slowness. waitForAppReady() does NOT fix it (grid genuinely absent). WHY AR-ROOM/AR-GRID (21-arrangement-flow) PASS: they hard-goto /arrange as the FIRST nav (slot matches page.tsx) and never hard-goto BACK after room-select; AR-ROOM relies on the modal's router.back() (SOFT nav, re-matches @grid/page.tsx). RECOMMENDED FIX (fresh session — verify Next16 parallel-route default.tsx semantics via next-devtools nextjs_docs first): make @grid/default.tsx render <GridSlot/> (same as page.tsx) so hard-nav/refresh shows the grid — ALSO fixes a REAL user bug (refresh/deep-link arrange after room-select = blank grid). Alt (test-only): navigate back via soft nav not hard goto. DEFERRED as structural parallel-route change (session-tail regression risk; this RSC/parallel-route area bit twice before — DndContext id, component={Link}). #247 grade-slot helper is sound and the keeper.

title: React Doctor: fix ERROR-severity findings (254 across 8 rules)
description: React Doctor score 42/100. Scope: ERROR-severity only, full repo. Tiers: A safe mechanical (no-nested-component-definition x2, jsx-key x1); B react-compiler-no-manual-memoization x156 GATED on not-in-compiler-bailout-component; C server-auth-actions x11 (security, triage FPs); D behavior-sensitive (set-state-in-effect x21, no-adjust-state-on-prop-change x7, react-hooks-js/todo x55 compiler bailout, nextjs-async-client-component x1). Working-tree edits only, no commit per react.doctor playbook. Typecheck after each tier.
notes:
SESSION 3 (bailout tier): INVESTIGATED + RESOLVED.

DONE: teacher-table/page.tsx (3 memo) - was a CLEAN file missed in the sweep, not actually bailout. Swept (useMemo->IIFE). typecheck+lint clean.

BAILOUT TIER DECISION: LEAVE memos, documented as compiler-bailout FALSE POSITIVES (user-approved, mirrors the 8 security FPs).
- Root cause: these ~13 files bail because of idiomatic try/catch/finally in async submit/fetch handlers (React Compiler 'Handle TryStatement with a finalizer' / ArrowFunctionExpression-reorder limitations). In a bailed component the compiler memoizes NOTHING, so the useMemo/useCallback are LOAD-BEARING (correct, not redundant). react-doctor's no-manual-memoization is a FALSE POSITIVE in bailed components.
- VERIFICATION TRAP: react-doctor --diff does NOT suppress memo findings in bailed components (AddProgramDialog reports BOTH memo + todo on the same component). So --diff can't confirm an un-bail fix; only the 'todo' finding disappearing is a valid signal. Do NOT trust --diff on the memo rule for these.
- Clearing would require rewriting error handling (move setX(false) out of finally) to un-bail, then remove memos = NET-ZERO runtime (dialogs/forms, not hot paths) + regression risk. Not worth it. Evidence files: useConflictResolution.ts (finally w/o catch - can't fix cleanly), useRoomAvailability.ts, AddProgramDialog/AddGradeLevel/AddTeacher/AddSubjectDialog, BulkLockModal, QuickAssignmentPanel, HeaderClient, TeacherAssignmentPage, useSubjectAssignment, dashboard/page, SemesterAnalyticsDashboard, student-table/page.
- ACCEPTED FP: PublishReadinessCard.tsx:37 fetchReadiness useCallback - kept (useEffect dep; removing triggers no-effect-with-fresh-deps).

REMAINING (Tier D behavior-sensitive, separate concern): set-state-in-effect x21, no-adjust-state-on-prop-change x7, react-hooks-js/todo bailouts x55 (= the try/finally root causes above), nextjs-async-client x1.

title: E2E: wizard copyAssignments toggle carries teacher assignments
description:
Follow-up to a77. Action + UI wiring landed with unit tests. Need Playwright E2E coverage for end-to-end carryover behavior.

Scope:

- Two scenarios:
  1. Create new semester with copyFrom=<existing>, copyAssignments=true → navigate to /management/teacher-assignment for new year/sem → assignment rows visible without manual click.
  2. Create new semester with copyFrom=<existing>, copyAssignments=false → target page empty.

- Spec runs under pnpm dev:e2e (loads .env.test, hits local docker postgres :5433).
- No reliance on remote Prisma Accelerate.
notes: Depends on a77 implementation already merged. mapAssignmentsForTarget unit tests in src/features/teaching-assignment/domain/utils/copy-assignments.test.ts cover pure logic. CopyPreviousStep.test.tsx covers checkbox behavior. E2E proves transaction-level integration.

title: Add copy-assignments option to CreateSemesterWizard
description:
Wizard currently copies only timeslots/config when cloning previous semester. Caption on CopyPreviousStep explicitly states teacher assignments are NOT copied. Per-grade copy exists in /management/teacher-assignment via copyAssignmentsAction but requires admin to repeat per grade after semester creation.

Goal: optional all-grades carryover at semester creation time, default-off, opt-in toggle.

Scope:
1. Add copyAssignments boolean to CreateSemesterContext + CopyPreviousStep checkbox (default false).
2. Thread copyAssignments through createSemesterWithTimeslotsAction + createSemesterAction inputs.
3. In semester.actions.ts create flow, after timeslots copy, if copyAssignments=true: list all gradelevel IDs that have rows in source (TeacherID,GradeID,SubjectCode,AcademicYear,Semester) tuple → loop copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester per grade.
4. Update caption to reflect new toggle.
5. Surface count summary in wizard success state.

Notes:
- copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester signature: (sourceGradeId, sourceSem, sourceYear, targetGradeId, targetSem, targetYear). Same-grade mapping correct (gradelevel labels persist across years).
- bulkAssignTeachers upserts on (SubjectCode,GradeID,Semester,AcademicYear,TeacherID) — safe idempotent.
- handleCopyFromPrevious (TeacherAssignmentPage.tsx:40) prev-sem math reusable: SEM_1→prev SEM_2 prev year, SEM_2→prev SEM_1 same year.
- Workload validation (teacher-validation.service) NOT run on copy path. Decide: validate post-copy and warn, or accept that source was already valid.
design:
Default OFF preserves current safe behavior (staffing changes between semesters common — retired teachers, new subject mix). Toggle gives power-user shortcut without masking changes silently.

Reuse copyAssignmentsAction rather than new bulk variant: existing action revalidates /management/teacher-assignment + invalidates teachers/stats caches. Loop in server action; surface aggregate count.

Alternative considered: new bulkCopyAssignmentsForSemesterAction taking sourceConfigId+targetConfigId, single repository call iterating grades server-side. Cleaner but duplicates loop logic. Reject unless wizard adds rollback semantics (then transaction matters).
acceptance_criteria:
TDD-first: every production change preceded by failing test, verified RED before GREEN.

Test order (red before any prod code):
1. Unit (domain/repo): copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester loop semantics — given source has 2 grades × 3 subjects, target gets 6 rows with matching (GradeID, SubjectCode, TeacherID, TeachHour). Mock prisma only at boundary; prefer integration with test DB.
2. Unit: prev-sem math — SEM_1 target → SEM_2 prev year source; SEM_2 target → SEM_1 same year source.
3. Unit: idempotent re-run — running copy twice produces same row count, no duplicate (upsert via bulkAssignTeachers).
4. Unit: empty source → target unchanged, count=0, no error.
5. Action: createSemesterWithTimeslotsAction with copyAssignments=true threads through and returns aggregate count in result.
6. Action: copyAssignments=false (default) leaves teachers_responsibility empty for target.
7. Component: CopyPreviousStep renders checkbox only when copyFrom selected; default unchecked.
8. Component: caption text no longer claims teacher data uncopied unconditionally.
9. E2E (Playwright, pnpm dev:e2e): wizard flow with copyAssignments=true → /management/teacher-assignment for target year/sem shows rows without manual click.
10. E2E: wizard flow with copyAssignments=false → target page shows empty assignments.

Definition of done:
- Each box above has corresponding test committed BEFORE its prod change, with git log showing test commit preceding implementation commit (or single commit where test diff precedes impl diff and was verified to fail).
- pnpm test green. pnpm test:e2e green. pnpm typecheck green. pnpm lint green.
- Output pristine: no warnings, no console errors in E2E run.
notes:
TDD discipline reminder:
- Write test, run, watch FAIL with expected message. If pass on first run → testing existing behavior, fix test.
- Minimal GREEN code only. No options/flags not driven by a test.
- Refactor only when green. Don't add behavior in refactor.
- No mocks unless unavoidable. Prisma boundary OK to stub; domain logic uses real code.
- Skipping a test because "obvious" = rationalization. Iron law: no prod code without failing test first.
close_reason: Implementation + unit tests landed. E2E split to clr.

title: Happy-path publish e2e: dedicated minimal MOE-compliant seed mode + Playwright project
description: Split from z45 (block test shipped there). The clean-publish happy path (config flips DRAFT->PUBLISHED through the readiness gate with status=ready) cannot run against the demo seed: checkPublishReadiness + findFullConfigData use UNFILTERED queries (program.findMany({IsActive:true}), gradelevel.findMany()), so readiness is a GLOBAL property. All 18 seeded programs fail MOE on MISSING learning areas (M4/5/6-SCI have no ARTS/CAREER program_subject rows) + systemic under-crediting (juniors ~2.5 vs 5 required) — not a MinCredits bump. AND every gradelevel must be 100% scheduled in the term. So a green happy-path needs a dedicated minimal-seed MODE that TRUNCATEs and seeds a tiny compliant world, wired through a SEPARATE Playwright project so it doesn't break specs that expect the full 18-grade school.

Then global readiness=ready. New Playwright project with its own globalSetup seeding this mode (env flag e.g. SEED_PUBLISH_HAPPY=true). value: Parallel worktree-agent fan-out + beads = merge conflicts. The bd pre-commit hook auto-exports .beads/issues.jsonl into EVERY commit, so each agent's PR carries a different issues.jsonl; when 2+ land, later PRs conflict on that file (globals.css etc merge clean). Mitigations: (1) claim/update/close issues ONLY from the parent session, tell sub-agents to never run bd or touch .beads/; (2) resolve the jsonl merge conflict with 'git checkout --theirs -- .beads/issues.jsonl' (Dolt is source of truth, hook re-exports fresh on next commit); (3) if checkout/merge complains 'local changes would be overwritten' it's usually STAGED (M in col 1) from a prior add — reset with 'git checkout HEAD -- .beads/issues.jsonl' first. Also: fresh agent worktrees have no node_modules, so they pnpm install + prisma generate before typecheck; and harness locks agent worktrees (cannot 'git worktree remove' without -f -f) — leave them for harness reaping.

test env: .env contains PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL pointing at remote Prisma Postgres. prisma/seed.ts uses 'import dotenv/config' which auto-loads .env even under 'dotenv -e .env.test'. src/lib/prisma.ts prismaClientSingleton prefers Accelerate URL over DATABASE_URL when set. .env.test and .env.test.local now explicitly set PRISMA_DATABASE_URL= and ACCELERATE_URL= empty to override .env and force the local docker postgres on :5433. If you ever see seed fail with USER_ALREADY_EXISTS while docker timetable-test-db shows User table empty, suspect this leak again.

RSC pitfall (Next16 + MUI): <Button component={Link}> (or any MUI 'component=' prop = a component reference) inside a SERVER component crashes at RUNTIME with 'Functions cannot be passed directly to Client Components' — RSC can't serialize the function. pnpm build does NOT catch it; only hit on navigation/e2e. Fix: use a plain href string (Button renders an anchor) or extract a 'use client' wrapper. component={Link} is fine inside client components. OCCURRENCES: (1) curriculum step page, commit d07e4eb9; (2) arrange/layout.tsx empty-state (server component), fixed PR #234 2026-05-29 — found via 8kp e2e WebServer log. To find all: grep 'component={Link}' and check which files lack 'use client' (those are the bugs). This was a top contributor to chronic e2e redness (8kp).

dnd-kit + SSR hydration: a <DndContext> WITHOUT a stable id prop logs a hydration mismatch on hard page load — aria-describedby='DndDescribedBy-N' differs server vs client (dnd-kit auto-generates it from an SSR-unstable counter). Build/typecheck do NOT catch it; only visible as a console error on a full SSR load (soft SPA nav hides it). Fix: pass <DndContext id="...">. Fixed in arrange ArrangeDndProvider (id='arrange-dnd', PR #239). If you add a new DndContext anywhere, give it an id.
